title: Test bud copying from rootdir
body:
Following up on #1511 with a test. (Thanks for merging 1511 by the way!)

Verified to fail prior to #1511 merging, and to pass after it was merged.

Also updated an unrelated test to put it's `output.iid` scratch file into TESTDIR. This both seemed like a common pattern for these tests and avoids leaving around a scratch file to dirty up the working directory after running tests locally.

Also unrelated, but it was a bit tricky to get these tests running. It wasn't clear how to run them or what dependencies needed to be installed (to further complicate things I'm using Fedora Silverblue where `go` and other build tools are inside a toolbox podman container). If I have some time I'll try to PR a testing section for  `CONTRIBUTING.md`

comments.body:
Looks like CI failed for an unrelated issue:
`The command "./hack/tree_status.sh" failed and exited with 1 during .`
@danstiner Yes, it seems like one of the tools we run for testing has changed.  I added a fix for this in one of my PRs.  Hopefully it will get merged this morning and then  you can rebase.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 0e30da6) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
No problem! Just let me know once things are reliable again and I'll rebase

I had already rebased onto latest master. That did fix the tree status issue! But I hit some other kind of build failure.

Failing test:

Sadly I think you need to rebase again.  The kubernetes/pause container has moved.  The latest test code has it in the new location.
No problem, and it looks like things are passing this time around!

:pushpin: Commit a478aab has been approved by `TomSweeneyRedHat`
